rationale: The citing paper discusses Sethuraman, Tellis, and Briesch (2011) in its core literature review on own-channel advertising effects, reporting its key empirical generalization (average short-term elasticity around .12) and its conclusion that effectiveness declined over time. These results are used as a benchmark to frame the paper's motivation that newer media environments and channels require broader cross-channel assessment. So the citation does more than a passing mention: it informs the empirical framing and positioning of the contribution.
relation_description: The citing paper uses the cited meta-analysis as an empirical baseline on traditional own-channel advertising elasticity and trend decline, then positions its cross-channel online/offline analysis as addressing questions beyond that benchmark.
